content.question: Do Hungary's rising SNI identification rates reflect a genuine increase in underlying developmental/learning need, or primarily changes in diagnostic practice, school-entry/readiness policy, or financial incentives embedded in special-education funding or placement rules?
content.reduction_path: Conduct longitudinal comparison of cohort-based prevalence estimates (e.g., same birth cohorts assessed at consistent ages across years) versus year-on-year identification totals; separately analyze changes in diagnostic tool usage, school-entry age policy, and special-education funding allocation formulas; interview municipal education administrators about tier-assignment decision-making.
